name: The Sohra (Cherrapunji) Circuit
description: The classic southern ridge — plunging waterfalls, deep limestone caves, and views that drop into Bangladesh.
travelReality: This is Meghalaya at its most dramatic. Waterfalls cascade off cliffs that fall into the plains below. The Double Decker Root Bridge is a full-day commitment on its own — plan accordingly.

name: The Dawki & Mawlynnong Circuit
description: The eastern fork toward the Bangladesh border — rivers so clear you can see every pebble, and villages that define hospitality.
travelReality: A world apart from Sohra. The Umngot River runs crystal-clear at Dawki, and Mawlynnong village has earned global recognition for its cleanliness and community-led tourism.

name: The Jaintia Hills (Jowai) Circuit
description: East into the Jaintia Hills — a land of blue pools, monoliths older than memory, and roads less traveled.
travelReality: Jaw-dropping turquoise water holes and massive standing stones that have marked this landscape for centuries. Less crowded, more raw. Do not attempt to combine this with other routes in one day — the distance demands respect.

name: The Shillong Central & Local Circuit
description: The pulse of Shillong — lakes, forests, lookouts, and local life within arm's reach.
travelReality: Ideal for an arrival day, a relaxed rest between mountain routes, or a final farewell ride before you leave. Shillong reveals its quieter side here.
